leftControls: number of units to add input

objective: Atomic roadmap + Linear execution structure for feedback package with cross-TERP propagation and QA gates.
dag: W4 (Pick & Pack + Orders propagation) -> W5 (Accounting + Clients) -> W6 (Inventory + release readiness)

tasks.title: Sales density baseline on order workspace
tasks.goal: Increase rows visible before first scroll while preserving required controls.
tasks.inputs: R-001, C001-C006, resolved default mode context
acceptance: Desktop/laptop shows materially more rows before first scroll.
Right summary column remains readable and complete.
No submission-critical control is hidden.
tasks.rollback: Feature flag `sales_density_v2`; revert layout token commit if scan path regresses.

tasks.title: Sales filter/sort/quick-actions contract restoration
tasks.goal: Restore predictable filter/sort/quick-action placement and persistence.
acceptance: Filter controls are visible or one-click discoverable.
Sort state persists through status mutation and refresh.
Quick actions stay in stable location with keyboard order preserved.
tasks.rollback: Toggle back to legacy toolbar placement via config map.

tasks.title: Order composer hierarchy cleanup
tasks.goal: Reorder customer context and order execution blocks for scan efficiency.
acceptance: Customer context remains grouped at top.
Order preview and summary are in intended relative order.
Low-value whitespace is repurposed with actionable content.
tasks.owner: Product decomposition + acceptance contract lane + map-interview-to-roadmap
tasks.rollback: Restore prior section ordering map and gate with snapshot diff.

tasks.title: Left-side units input + quick-add button placement
tasks.goal: Place confirmed controls on left side while keeping inline edit speed.
tasks.inputs: R-003, C013-C016, resolved controls list
acceptance: `Number of units to add` input is left-default and editable inline.
`+` button is left-default, paired, and keyboard reachable.
Placement remains stable after refresh/layout mode switch.
tasks.rollback: Fallback to previous control slot map under `sales_left_controls_v2` flag.

tasks.title: Quantity step=1 hardening in sales composer
tasks.goal: Enforce deterministic integer quantity behavior for increment, typing, and paste.
acceptance: Increment/decrement changes by exactly 1.
Decimal/invalid pasted values normalize or reject deterministically.
Pricing recalculation remains correct after normalization.
tasks.rollback: Revert numeric input guard utility and re-enable legacy parser.

tasks.title: Client profile drawer deep-link to credit limit
tasks.goal: Open right drawer from client trigger and deep-link to credit limit editor.
acceptance: Client click opens right drawer (no full-route break).
Drawer lands at credit-limit section.
Saved changes reflect in active order context.
tasks.rollback: Disable deep-link routing param and restore previous client open behavior.

tasks.title: Pricing profile/rules drawer link near customer controls
tasks.goal: Match drawer pattern from credit workflow for pricing profile access.
acceptance: Pricing drawer uses same interaction contract as credit drawer.
Trigger is colocated with customer controls.
Permission checks gate pricing edits correctly.
tasks.rollback: Switch to read-only pricing drawer and disable edit affordances.

tasks.title: Pricing defaults to customer profile + dollar-first mode
tasks.goal: Default line-item pricing to profile and expose override source clearly.
acceptance: Customer pricing profile is default source when present.
Default markup mode is dollar-first.
Line-item override state is visibly labeled profile/manual.
tasks.rollback: Feature flag `pricing_profile_default_v2`; fallback to existing mode resolution.

tasks.title: Post-save return_to_origin contract enforcement
tasks.goal: Make drawer save outcomes deterministic and preserve failed-save context.
tasks.inputs: R-010, C050-C051, resolved policy return_to_origin
acceptance: Successful save returns to origin context deterministically.
Drawer close/reopen behavior is stable.
Failed save keeps edits and context intact.
tasks.rollback: Fallback to legacy drawer close policy with warning banner.

tasks.title: Field-specific validation guidance in sales pricing controls
tasks.goal: Replace vague validation with field-rule-fix messaging.
acceptance: Errors include exact field + failed rule + fix guidance.
Inline placement is adjacent to source control.
Summary bar mirrors blocking errors when needed.
tasks.rollback: Revert to previous validation copy map and disable new summary adapter.

tasks.title: Mode-aware primary CTA semantics (non-shipping default)
tasks.goal: Set Make Payment as primary in non-shipping mode with role-aware visibility.
tasks.inputs: R-011, C052-C055, resolved non-shipping default mode
acceptance: Non-shipping mode primary CTA is Make Payment with accounting handoff.
Shipping-enabled mode keeps explicit shipping CTA.
CTA visibility follows RBAC and mode rules.
tasks.rollback: Restore previous CTA resolver and disable mode override.

tasks.title: Status controls + filtered-list reliability in core sales flow
tasks.goal: Prevent blank-list regressions after status mutation and explain filtered-out movement.
tasks.inputs: R-012, C056-C062, resolved non-shipping transition defaults
acceptance: Status updates available in both requested locations.
Lists do not blank unexpectedly after status change.
Filtered-out item explanation is deterministic.
tasks.rollback: Revert transition map patch and re-enable last known stable filter refresh path.

tasks.title: Primary entry CTA rename and discoverability (`New Sale`)
tasks.goal: Unify entry action naming and remove conflicting synonyms.
acceptance: Primary entrypoint label is New Sale in target flow.
Conflicting labels in same context are removed.
Placement and helper affordance improve first-run discoverability.
tasks.rollback: Restore old label map under i18n token rollback.

tasks.title: A-001 propagation on Orders
tasks.goal: Propagate Filter/sort/search + quick-action placement contract to Orders without breaking local task speed or keyboard flow.

acceptance: Filter/sort/search and quick actions follow shared placement contract on Orders.
Sort/filter state remains stable after mutation/refresh.
Keyboard focus order remains deterministic.

startHere: Execute Wave 1 core foundation tickets (CF-001, CF-002, CF-003) in parallel lanes where possible.
Run Wave 2 composer/drawer contract tickets, keeping CF-003 and CF-006 as hard dependency gates.
Execute Wave 3 semantics/reliability tickets; require RED-mode V4 gate for CF-011 and CF-012.
Propagate A-items to Pick & Pack + Orders in Wave 4, then Accounting + Clients in Wave 5, then Inventory in Wave 6.
Close each wave only after QA evidence packet is attached to parent and child Linear tickets.
